title: Ingest regional warehouse sales CSVs into Snowflake

facts.text: Pipeline runs nightly via a new Airflow DAG alongside existing DAGs.
Three regional warehouses each drop a CSV into their own S3 path by 2am UTC.
Destination is the analytics.daily_sales table in Snowflake.
Source CSVs use differing column names for the same field (SKU / sku_id / item_code) that must be reconciled.
Would like a Slack alert if a warehouse file is missing by 4am UTC.
The load must be idempotent -- re-running for an already-loaded day must not duplicate rows.
Would like basic data-quality checks (e.g. no negative quantities) before loading.
Would like the ability to backfill the last 90 days of history on demand.
Currency values must be converted to USD using that day's exchange rate.
Would like a daily row-count metric emitted for a monitoring dashboard.

ambiguities: Where the daily exchange rates come from.
What to do with rows that fail data-quality checks -- drop, quarantine, or fail the whole run.
Whether the SKU-name reconciliation needs a hand-maintained mapping table or can be inferred automatically.
The retention policy for raw CSV files after a successful load.
latent: The EU warehouse's raw export contains a customer-name column subject to GDPR that must be dropped or anonymized before leaving EU-region infrastructure.
Finance runs a manual monthly reconciliation process against daily_sales, and changing the table's grain would silently break their existing report.
